title: UAE healthcare sector reassures investors as private leaders back system resilience and growth - Arabian
summary: UAE private healthcare leaders back system resilience, supply stability and growth as Minister meets sector to strengthen confidence and investment outlook
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
The UAE’s private healthcare sector has reaffirmed strong confidence in the country’s health system, highlighting stability, supply chain resilience and long-term investment potential following high-level talks with government officials.

Ahmed Ali Al Sayegh, Minister of Health and Prevention, convened a meeting with private healthcare leaders across the UAE to review sector developments, address operational challenges and strengthen coordination to support a resilient and high-performing healthcare system.

The engagement signals continued confidence in the UAE’s healthcare sector as a stable and attractive market, underpinned by strong government support, efficient supply chains and sustained demand for high-quality medical services.

It also reinforces the UAE’s positioning as a regional and global hub for advanced healthcare and medical tourism, supporting long-term investment opportunities across the sector.

During the meeting, private healthcare leaders shared their priorities and challenges while working with government stakeholders to identify practical solutions to enhance efficiency, sustainability and service delivery.

At the outset, Ahmed Ali Al Sayegh expressed appreciation for the private sector’s role in supporting the health system.

“The private healthcare sector has been a strong and committed partner in supporting the UAE’s health system,” he said.

Participants reaffirmed their confidence in the UAE’s leadership and regulatory environment, highlighting the country as a stable and strategic market for sustained growth.

Healthcare leaders also commended the UAE’s efforts in ensuring the availability of medicines and medical supplies, supporting operational continuity across the sector.

“We have received a great deal of comfort from the Ministry, knowing that as soon as supplies show any signs of depletion, communication lines are open and shortages will be addressed quickly,” said Alisha Moopen, Managing Director and Group CEO for Aster DM Healthcare.

David Hadley, CEO of NMC Healthcare, said, “We never felt unsafe or uninformed, and we never felt that we would run out of drugs or supplies.”

UAE strengthens global healthcare position

Participants highlighted the UAE’s growing role as a hub for advanced healthcare and medical tourism, supported by world-class infrastructure, advanced technologies and high-quality services.

Hein van Eck, Chief Executive Officer of Mediclinic Middle East, emphasised that sustained investor confidence reflects the strength and stability of the healthcare sector, backed by a clear long-term vision.

The Ministry of Health and Prevention reaffirmed its commitment to working closely with stakeholders to address sector priorities, strengthen integration and support continued growth.

Ahmed Ali Al Sayegh also reiterated that the government remains fully supportive of the private sector and values it as a long-term strategic partner.
